perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Invoices and Payment. Antares shall submit invoices to AMAG for Products, sample Products and Trainers pursuant to Section 3 and Safety Stock pursuant to Section 3.6, which invoices shall include reasonable detail for the amounts included therein. Unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Parties, AMAG shall pay the undisputed portion of each invoice within [***] of receipt of such invoice. Antares shall submit invoices to AMAG for Products, sample Products and Trainers pursuant to Section 3 and Safety Stock pursuant to Section 3.6, which invoices shall include reasonable detail for the amounts included therein. Unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Parties, AMAG shall pay the undisputed portion of each invoice within 15 business days of receipt of such invoice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by providing **two different payment deadlines for the same invoices within the same Section 4.2**. The first sentence specifies payment within '[***]' days, while the second specifies **15 business days**. This contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the payment terms and which deadline should be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Invoices and Payment. Antares shall submit invoices to AMAG for Products, sample Products and Trainers pursuant to Section 3 and Safety Stock pursuant to Section 3.6, which invoices shall include reasonable detail for the amounts included therein. Unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Parties, AMAG shall pay the undisputed portion of each invoice within [***] of receipt of such invoice. Antares shall submit invoices to AMAG for Products, sample Products and Trainers pursuant to Section 3 and Safety Stock pursuant to Section 3.6, which invoices shall include reasonable detail for the amounts included therein. Unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Parties, AMAG shall pay the undisputed portion of each invoice within 15 business days of receipt of such invoice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within Section 4.2 regarding the payment deadline for invoices. The original '[***]' is replaced with '15 business days,' and now both payment terms exist simultaneously in the same section. This creates uncertainty and inconsistency regarding the actual payment deadline, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Orders and Forecasts. (a) Rolling Forecasts. On or before the [***] after the Effective Date, AMAG shall provide Antares with an updated written [***] rolling forecast of the volume of Product, sample Product and Trainers that AMAG then anticipates will be required to be produced and delivered to AMAG during [***] (the "Forecast"). The initial Forecast is attached hereto as Exhibit H. [***] of each Forecast shall constitute a firm order and be a binding commitment on AMAG to purchase the volume of Product, sample Product and Trainers set forth therein (the "Binding Forecast").
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Orders and Forecasts. (a) Rolling Forecasts. On or before the [***] after the Effective Date, AMAG shall provide Antares with an updated written [***] rolling forecast of the volume of Product, sample Product and Trainers that AMAG then anticipates will be required to be produced and delivered to AMAG during [***] (the "Forecast"). The initial Forecast is attached hereto as Exhibit H. The entirety of each Forecast shall constitute a firm order and be a binding commitment on AMAG to purchase the volume of Product, sample Product and Trainers set forth therein (the "Binding Forecast"). [***] of each Forecast shall constitute a firm order and be a binding commitment on AMAG to purchase the volume of Product, sample Product and Trainers set forth therein (the "Binding Forecast").
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the **'entirety of each Forecast'** is a binding commitment, then immediately contradicting this by saying that **'[***] of each Forecast'** is the binding commitment. This introduces uncertainty about which portion of the forecast AMAG is actually obligated to purchase, potentially leading to disputes regarding order volumes and financial commitments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Orders and Forecasts. (a) Rolling Forecasts. On or before the [***] after the Effective Date, AMAG shall provide Antares with an updated written [***] rolling forecast of the volume of Product, sample Product and Trainers that AMAG then anticipates will be required to be produced and delivered to AMAG during [***] (the "Forecast"). The initial Forecast is attached hereto as Exhibit H. The entirety of each Forecast shall constitute a firm order and be a binding commitment on AMAG to purchase the volume of Product, sample Product and Trainers set forth therein (the "Binding Forecast"). [***] of each Forecast shall constitute a firm order and be a binding commitment on AMAG to purchase the volume of Product, sample Product and Trainers set forth therein (the "Binding Forecast").
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by first stating that the 'entirety' of the forecast is binding and then immediately stating that only '[***]' (some unspecified portion) of the forecast is binding. This creates a direct conflict regarding the scope of AMAG's purchase obligation, which contradicts itself within the text. The contradictory language makes it unclear what portion of the forecast constitutes a binding commitment.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 Minimum Orders. The quantity of Products, sample Products or Trainers (as the case may be) ordered by AMAG from Antares in each shipment (as set forth in a Purchase Order) must be equal to or greater than [***] units for each type of Product, sample Product and Trainers ordered. Such minimum order quantity may be updated from time to time by a mutual written agreement of the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 Minimum Orders. The quantity of Products, sample Products or Trainers (as the case may be) ordered by AMAG from Antares in each shipment (as set forth in a Purchase Order) must be equal to or greater than [***] units for each type of Product, sample Product and Trainers ordered. Such minimum order quantity is fixed and may not be updated from time to time by a mutual written agreement of the Parties. Such minimum order quantity may be updated from time to time by a mutual written agreement of the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by first stating the minimum order quantity is fixed and cannot be updated, then stating the quantity may be updated from time to time by agreement of both parties. This creates in-text contradiction in determining if the minimum order is a set number and if that quantity may be altered if both parties agree on an updated number.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Minimum Orders. The quantity of Products, sample Products or Trainers (as the case may be) ordered by AMAG from Antares in each shipment (as set forth in a Purchase Order) must be equal to or greater than [***] units for each type of Product, sample Product and Trainers ordered. Such minimum order quantity is fixed and may not be updated from time to time by a mutual written agreement of the Parties. Such minimum order quantity may be updated from time to time by a mutual written agreement of the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction within the same section (3.3). It first states that the minimum order quantity is fixed and cannot be updated, then immediately states that it can be updated by mutual agreement. This is a clear and undeniable contradiction within the text, making it unclear whether the minimum order quantity is fixed or subject to change. The changed text and the contradicted text are the same in this case, but the change in the original text, by adding contradicting statements to the same section, is what creates the contradiction.
